_meta.disclaimer: Evidence requirements are constructed for educational purposes. Customise for your engagement.

evidenceItems.name: Technology Risk Governance Framework Document
evidenceItems.description: Board-approved technology risk governance framework defining roles, responsibilities, committee structure, three lines of defence model, and reporting lines for capital market operations.
whatGoodLooksLike: Board-approved with annual review cycle
Covers three lines of defence for technology risk
Defines technology risk committee charter and membership
Aligned with SC-GL/6-2023 governance expectations
commonGaps: No formal governance framework document
Framework exists but not board-approved
Three lines of defence not articulated
No technology risk committee established

evidenceItems.name: CISO Appointment and Authority Records
evidenceItems.description: Evidence of CISO designation including appointment letter, reporting line documentation, scope of authority, resource allocation, and independence from IT operations.
whatGoodLooksLike: Formal appointment with defined scope and authority
Direct reporting to CEO or board committee
Independence from IT operations function
Adequate budget and headcount allocation documented
commonGaps: No dedicated CISO role — security under IT manager
CISO reports to CTO with no board access
Scope limited to operations without strategic mandate
Insufficient resources relative to entity size

evidenceItems.name: Technology Risk Policy Suite
evidenceItems.description: Complete set of board-approved technology risk management policies covering cybersecurity, data management, BCP, cloud, outsourcing, incident management, and other SC-GL/6-2023 domains.
whatGoodLooksLike: Complete coverage of all SC-GL/6-2023 domains
Board-approved with documented approval dates
Annual review cycle with version control
Communicated to all relevant personnel with acknowledgement records
commonGaps: Partial policy coverage — missing domains
Policies not board-approved
Stale policies with no review in over 12 months
No evidence of communication to staff

evidenceItems.name: Technology Risk Reports to Board
evidenceItems.description: Quarterly technology risk reports presented to the board or board committee, including KRIs, incident trends, compliance status, and emerging technology risks relevant to capital market operations.
whatGoodLooksLike: Quarterly reports with consistent structure
Includes KRIs, incident metrics, and compliance status
Board discussion and actions documented in minutes
Emerging technology risks addressed
commonGaps: Reports not produced regularly
Limited to incident summaries without risk context
No board discussion documented
KRIs not defined or tracked

evidenceItems.name: Cyber Threat Intelligence Programme Records
evidenceItems.description: Evidence of threat intelligence consumption, threat assessments specific to capital market threats, and participation in SC and industry threat sharing programmes.
whatGoodLooksLike: Subscribed to relevant threat intelligence feeds (government, industry, commercial)
Periodic threat assessments for capital market-specific threats
Threat hunting activities documented with findings
Active participation in SC/MyCERT threat sharing
commonGaps: No threat intelligence programme
Generic feeds with no capital market context
No threat hunting capability
No participation in industry sharing

evidenceItems.name: SOC Operations Evidence
evidenceItems.description: Evidence of security operations centre capabilities including monitoring coverage, SIEM deployment, detection rule tuning for capital market systems, and incident response metrics.
whatGoodLooksLike: 24/7 monitoring coverage for critical trading and settlement systems
Detection rules tuned for capital market-specific attack patterns
MTTD and MTTR metrics tracked and reported
Regular SOC effectiveness assessments conducted
commonGaps: Business hours monitoring only
Generic detection rules not tailored to market systems
No performance metrics tracked
No effectiveness assessment conducted

evidenceItems.name: Vulnerability Management Programme Records
evidenceItems.description: Vulnerability scan results, patch management SLAs, remediation tracking, and exception management for capital market technology systems.
whatGoodLooksLike: Regular scanning with full asset coverage including trading systems
Risk-based patch SLAs defined and tracked
Critical findings remediated within defined SLAs
Exception process documented with compensating controls
commonGaps: Infrequent or incomplete scanning
Trading systems excluded from scope
No defined patching SLAs
Findings not tracked to closure

evidenceItems.name: Network Security Architecture Documentation
evidenceItems.description: Network architecture diagrams showing segmentation between trading, settlement, market data, and corporate networks, with IDS/IPS deployment and access control evidence.
whatGoodLooksLike: Network diagrams showing segmentation of trading and settlement zones
IDS/IPS deployed at key boundaries for market infrastructure
Market data feed connectivity secured and documented
Annual architecture review against threat landscape
commonGaps: Flat network with no segmentation between trading and corporate
No IDS/IPS on market-facing interfaces
Market data feeds not secured
Architecture not reviewed in over 12 months

evidenceItems.name: Security Awareness Training Records
evidenceItems.description: Records of cybersecurity awareness training for staff handling capital market systems, including phishing simulations and role-specific training for trading and operations teams.
whatGoodLooksLike: Annual training for all staff with completion tracking
Role-specific modules for trading and operations personnel
Phishing simulation results tracked with improvement trends
Training content updated for current capital market threats
commonGaps: No regular training programme
Generic content not relevant to capital market roles
No phishing simulations conducted
Completion rates not tracked

evidenceItems.name: Technology Risk Assessment Reports
evidenceItems.description: Annual comprehensive technology risk assessment reports covering all capital market technology systems, with defined methodology aligned to SC-GL/6-2023 and enterprise risk framework.
whatGoodLooksLike: Annual assessment with defined methodology
Covers trading, settlement, market data, and support systems
Risk ratings aligned with enterprise risk framework
Treatment plans for high and critical risks
commonGaps: No formal risk assessment methodology
Assessment does not cover all technology domains
Not aligned with enterprise risk framework
No follow-up on treatment plans

evidenceItems.name: Algorithmic Trading Risk Control Evidence
evidenceItems.description: Documentation of pre-trade and post-trade risk controls for algorithmic trading systems, including kill switch capability, position limits, and market manipulation monitoring.
whatGoodLooksLike: Pre-trade risk checks documented (price limits, order size, position limits)
Kill switch tested and operational with documented procedures
Algorithm testing and approval records before production deployment
Market manipulation monitoring with alert thresholds defined
commonGaps: No pre-trade risk controls beyond basic order size limits
Kill switch not tested or non-functional
Algorithms deployed without formal testing or approval
No market manipulation monitoring for algo-generated orders

evidenceItems.name: Technology Change Risk Assessment Records
evidenceItems.description: Change risk assessment records demonstrating risk classification, approval gates, testing evidence, and rollback procedures for technology changes to capital market systems.
whatGoodLooksLike: Changes classified by risk level (critical, significant, standard)
Risk assessment completed for all critical and significant changes
Testing and approval gates documented before production deployment
Rollback procedures defined and tested for critical changes
commonGaps: No change risk classification
Changes deployed without risk assessment
No testing evidence for production deployments
Rollback procedures not defined or tested

evidenceItems.name: Technology Risk Register
evidenceItems.description: Maintained technology risk register showing identified risks, risk ratings, owners, treatment plans, and status tracking across all capital market technology domains.
whatGoodLooksLike: Comprehensive register covering all technology domains
Risk ratings with likelihood and impact scores
Named risk owners with accountability
Treatment plans tracked to completion with target dates
commonGaps: No centralised risk register
Incomplete coverage of technology domains
Risks identified but no treatment plans
Register not updated after assessments

evidenceItems.name: Cloud Risk Assessment Records
evidenceItems.description: Cloud-specific risk assessments for each cloud adoption, including shared responsibility matrices, CSP certification reviews, and multi-tenancy and data sovereignty analysis.
whatGoodLooksLike: Risk assessment completed per cloud engagement
Shared responsibility matrix documented for each CSP
CSP certifications (SOC 2, ISO 27001) reviewed and current
Data sovereignty implications assessed for client and market data
commonGaps: No cloud-specific risk assessment performed
Shared responsibility not documented
CSP certifications not reviewed or expired
Data sovereignty not considered for Malaysian client data

evidenceItems.name: Cloud Data Governance Documentation
evidenceItems.description: Evidence of data governance controls in cloud environments, including data classification implementation, encryption with customer-managed keys, access controls, and data residency verification.
whatGoodLooksLike: Data classification applied to all cloud-hosted data
Customer-managed encryption keys (BYOK/HYOK) for sensitive data
Data residency requirements enforced for client information in Malaysia
Cloud access monitored and audited with logs retained
commonGaps: Data not classified in cloud environments
Reliance on CSP-managed keys only
Data residency not verified or monitored
No access monitoring or audit trail for cloud data

evidenceItems.name: Cloud Exit Strategy Documentation
evidenceItems.description: Documented exit plans for material cloud services, including data portability assessment, migration procedures, alternative provider identification, and periodic testing of exit procedures.
whatGoodLooksLike: Exit plans documented for all material cloud deployments
Data extraction and migration procedures defined and tested
Alternative CSPs or on-premises fallback options identified
Open standards used where possible for portability
commonGaps: No exit planning for any cloud service
Data portability not assessed
Exit procedures never tested
Vendor lock-in with no alternative identified

evidenceItems.name: Vendor Risk Assessment Records
evidenceItems.description: Vendor risk tiering, due diligence reports, security questionnaire results, and ongoing monitoring evidence for technology service providers to capital market operations.
whatGoodLooksLike: All vendors tiered by risk level based on service criticality
Comprehensive due diligence for critical service providers
Security posture assessed through questionnaires and independent reviews
Annual reassessment for critical and high-risk vendors
commonGaps: No vendor risk tiering methodology
Due diligence limited to initial onboarding only
No security assessment of vendor posture
Cloud and fintech providers not assessed

evidenceItems.name: Outsourcing Governance Records
evidenceItems.description: Board approval records for material outsourcing, outsourcing policy, contractual standards (SLA, right-to-audit, data protection), and quarterly outsourcing risk reports to board.
whatGoodLooksLike: Board approval documented for material outsourcing arrangements
Outsourcing policy aligned with SC-GL/6-2023
Contracts include SLA, right-to-audit, data protection, and termination provisions
Quarterly outsourcing risk status reported to board
commonGaps: No board approval for outsourcing arrangements
No outsourcing policy
Contracts missing mandatory provisions (right-to-audit, data protection)
No regular reporting to board on outsourcing risk

evidenceItems.name: Vendor Performance Monitoring Reports
evidenceItems.description: SLA compliance reports, vendor performance dashboards, escalation records for SLA breaches, and periodic vendor relationship review minutes.
whatGoodLooksLike: Monthly SLA compliance reports for critical vendors
Automated SLA monitoring with exception alerting
Documented escalation for persistent underperformance
Periodic vendor satisfaction and relationship reviews
commonGaps: Reliance on vendor self-reporting only
No SLA tracking or compliance measurement
SLA breaches not escalated
No periodic vendor relationship reviews

evidenceItems.name: Fourth-Party Oversight Records
evidenceItems.description: Fourth-party register, sub-contractor disclosure records, supply chain risk assessments, and contractual provisions for sub-contractor oversight.
whatGoodLooksLike: Sub-contracting disclosed for all critical services
Fourth-party register maintained with risk assessment
Contracts include sub-contractor oversight provisions
Supply chain concentration risks identified and monitored
commonGaps: No visibility into fourth-party arrangements
Sub-contracting not required to be disclosed
No fourth-party risk assessment
Concentration risk not identified

evidenceItems.name: Third-Party Contracts Register
evidenceItems.description: Centralised register of all technology outsourcing contracts with key terms, expiry dates, renewal provisions, and mandatory clause compliance status.
whatGoodLooksLike: Complete register of all technology outsourcing contracts
Key terms tracked (SLA, right-to-audit, data protection, termination)
Expiry and renewal dates monitored
Mandatory SC-GL/6-2023 clause compliance verified
commonGaps: No centralised contract register
Key terms not tracked systematically
Contracts expired or auto-renewed without review
SC-GL/6-2023 mandatory provisions not verified

evidenceItems.name: Data Governance Framework and Classification Scheme
evidenceItems.description: Board-approved data governance framework, data classification scheme, data ownership register, and data quality standards for capital market and client data.
whatGoodLooksLike: Board-approved data governance framework with defined roles
Classification scheme applied to all market and client data assets
Data quality standards defined and monitored
PDPA compliance assessed and documented
commonGaps: No formal data governance framework
Data assets not classified
No data quality monitoring
PDPA compliance not assessed for capital market client data

evidenceItems.name: Market Data Integrity Controls Evidence
evidenceItems.description: Evidence of data validation checks on market data feeds, latency and accuracy monitoring, reconciliation processes, and data correction procedures for trading and settlement data.
whatGoodLooksLike: Automated validation checks on all market data feeds
Latency and accuracy metrics monitored against thresholds
Reconciliation processes for critical market data (prices, positions, trades)
Data correction and restatement procedures documented and tested
commonGaps: No validation checks on market data feeds
Latency not monitored for time-sensitive market data
No reconciliation between systems
Ad-hoc data corrections with no audit trail

evidenceItems.name: Client Data Protection Evidence
evidenceItems.description: Evidence of encryption controls, role-based access controls, DLP deployment, and data protection impact assessments for client information held by the capital market intermediary.
whatGoodLooksLike: Client data encrypted at rest and in transit with strong algorithms
Role-based access controls implemented and reviewed periodically
DLP tools deployed and monitoring client data flows
Data protection impact assessments conducted for new processing activities
commonGaps: Encryption not applied consistently to all client data stores
Access controls not reviewed — excessive privileges
No DLP deployed for client data
No data protection impact assessments conducted
evidenceItems.format: Configuration exports / DPIA reports / Access review records

evidenceItems.name: Data Retention and Disposal Records
evidenceItems.description: Data retention schedules, disposal procedures, and evidence of secure destruction for capital market records in compliance with SC record-keeping requirements.
whatGoodLooksLike: Retention schedules aligned with SC and PDPA requirements
Secure disposal procedures documented and followed
Destruction certificates maintained for disposed data
Periodic review of data stores against retention schedules
commonGaps: No formal retention schedule
Data retained indefinitely with no review
No secure disposal procedures
No destruction evidence maintained

evidenceItems.name: BCM Programme Documentation
evidenceItems.description: Business continuity management programme documentation including BIA for capital market functions, BCP covering trading, clearing, settlement, and custody, with defined RTO and RPO.
whatGoodLooksLike: BIA completed for all capital market functions
BCP covers trading, clearing, settlement, and custody operations
RTO and RPO defined for all market infrastructure components
BCP coordinated with Bursa Malaysia and clearing houses
commonGaps: No BIA or BIA does not cover market-specific functions
BCP generic — not tailored to capital market operations
RTO/RPO not defined for trading and settlement systems
No coordination with market infrastructure operators

evidenceItems.name: Trading System Resilience Evidence
evidenceItems.description: Evidence of trading system redundancy, failover architecture, capacity planning with headroom for market volatility, and alternative trading channel arrangements.
whatGoodLooksLike: Redundant trading infrastructure with automatic failover documented
Capacity planning includes headroom for peak volatility events
Failover tested regularly with results documented
Alternative trading channels identified for primary system failures
commonGaps: Single trading system with no redundancy
No capacity planning for market volatility spikes
Failover procedures not tested
No alternative trading channels available
evidenceItems.format: Architecture diagrams / Failover test reports / Capacity reports

evidenceItems.name: DR Testing Reports
evidenceItems.description: Disaster recovery test reports for critical capital market systems, including participation in Bursa Malaysia or SC-coordinated industry-wide DR exercises, with gap remediation evidence.
whatGoodLooksLike: Annual DR testing covering all critical market systems
Participation in industry-wide DR exercises as required
RTO/RPO validated during testing with results documented
Gaps identified and remediated with tracked actions
commonGaps: No DR testing for trading or settlement systems
Industry DR exercises not participated in
RTO/RPO not validated during tests
Test findings not remediated

evidenceItems.name: Settlement Continuity Arrangements
evidenceItems.description: Documentation of alternative settlement procedures, coordination arrangements with Bursa Malaysia Depository, manual fallback procedures, and communication protocols with clearing participants.
whatGoodLooksLike: Alternative settlement procedures documented for system failures
Coordination with Bursa Malaysia Depository formalised
Manual settlement fallback procedures tested periodically
Communication protocols with clearing and depository participants maintained
commonGaps: No alternative settlement procedures defined
No coordination with depository for disruption scenarios
Manual fallback never tested
No communication protocols for settlement disruptions

evidenceItems.name: Incident Response Plan
evidenceItems.description: Technology incident response plan specific to capital market operations, with classification matrix, escalation procedures, containment and recovery procedures, and RACI matrix.
whatGoodLooksLike: Plan reviewed and updated at least annually
Incident classification with severity levels for market operations
Clear RACI matrix for incident response team
Escalation procedures include SC notification triggers
commonGaps: No documented incident response plan
Plan not tailored to capital market operations
Roles and responsibilities unclear
SC notification triggers not defined

evidenceItems.name: SC Incident Notification Records
evidenceItems.description: Records of incident notifications submitted to SC Malaysia, including notification templates, submission timelines, SC acknowledgements, and compliance with SC-GL/6-2023 notification requirements.
whatGoodLooksLike: Notification criteria and thresholds defined per SC-GL/6-2023
Notification workflow with defined timelines documented
Templates prepared with all required information fields
Notification submissions and SC responses tracked
commonGaps: Notification criteria not defined
Manual ad-hoc notification with inconsistent timing
No templates — each notification created from scratch
Submissions not tracked — no confirmation of SC receipt

evidenceItems.name: Cyber Incident Coordination Evidence
evidenceItems.description: Evidence of coordination mechanisms with market participants, SC, MyCERT, and industry peers during cyber incidents, including communication protocols and threat intelligence sharing.
whatGoodLooksLike: Communication protocols established with regulators and market participants
Active participation in industry incident coordination mechanisms
Threat intelligence shared with SC and peer institutions
Post-incident reviews conducted with coordination partners
commonGaps: No coordination mechanisms established
No communication protocols with market participants
No threat intelligence sharing
Incidents handled in isolation without peer coordination

evidenceItems.name: Incident Post-Mortem Reports
evidenceItems.description: Structured post-incident review reports with root cause analysis, lessons learned, improvement actions, and evidence of action tracking to closure.
whatGoodLooksLike: Post-mortem conducted within defined timeframe after incident closure
Root cause and contributing factors identified
Lessons learned documented and shared
Improvement actions tracked to closure with named owners
commonGaps: No post-mortem process
Informal verbal debrief only — nothing documented
Root cause not identified
Actions identified but not tracked to closure

evidenceItems.name: Incident Response Exercise Records
evidenceItems.description: Records of tabletop exercises and incident simulations conducted, including scenarios, participants, findings, and improvements implemented.
whatGoodLooksLike: At least annual tabletop exercise with realistic capital market scenarios
Senior management participation including CISO and Compliance
SC notification procedures tested during exercises
Exercise findings incorporated into plan updates
commonGaps: No exercises conducted
Exercises do not include SC notification simulation
Senior management does not participate
Findings not used to improve the plan

evidenceItems.name: Technology Audit Programme and Reports
evidenceItems.description: Risk-based technology audit plan, audit reports covering all key technology domains, audit committee reporting, and findings remediation tracker.
whatGoodLooksLike: Risk-based audit plan covering all SC-GL/6-2023 domains
Qualified technology auditors with capital market experience engaged
Findings reported to board audit committee
Remediation tracked to closure with defined SLAs
commonGaps: Technology not included in audit plan
Auditors lack technology or capital market expertise
Findings not reported to audit committee
Remediation not tracked — findings remain open

evidenceItems.name: Regulatory Examination Readiness Evidence
evidenceItems.description: Self-assessment records against SC-GL/6-2023, standing evidence library status, examination response team designation, and tracker for prior examination findings.
whatGoodLooksLike: Periodic self-assessments against SC-GL/6-2023 requirements
Standing evidence library maintained and current
Examination response team and protocols defined
Prior examination findings tracked and addressed
commonGaps: No self-assessments conducted
Evidence gathered only when examination announced
No designated response team
Prior findings not tracked or remediated

evidenceItems.name: Penetration Testing Reports
evidenceItems.description: Annual penetration testing reports from qualified independent testers, covering trading systems, client portals, market connectivity, with remediation tracking for critical and high findings.
whatGoodLooksLike: Annual testing by qualified independent testers (CREST/OSCP)
Scope covers critical market systems and client-facing applications
Critical and high findings remediated within defined SLAs
Results reported to board audit committee
commonGaps: No regular penetration testing
Trading systems excluded from scope
Critical findings not remediated in timely manner
Results not reported to audit committee

evidenceItems.name: Algorithm Governance Records
evidenceItems.description: Algorithm approval process documentation, testing records (backtesting, simulation), production monitoring evidence, and periodic re-approval records for algorithmic trading systems.
whatGoodLooksLike: Formal approval process documented for each algorithm
Testing records including backtesting and simulation results
Production monitoring with anomaly detection and performance metrics
Periodic re-approval with performance review conducted
commonGaps: No formal approval process for algorithms
Insufficient testing before production deployment
No production monitoring of algorithm behaviour
No periodic review or re-approval process
evidenceItems.format: Approval records / Test reports / Monitoring dashboards

evidenceItems.name: AI/ML Risk Management Records
evidenceItems.description: Model risk assessments for AI/ML applications in trading and compliance, model validation and testing records, bias assessments, and explainability documentation.
whatGoodLooksLike: Model risk assessment for each AI/ML application in capital market operations
Model validation and testing procedures documented and followed
Algorithmic bias assessed and mitigated for client-facing applications
Explainability requirements defined and met for AI/ML-driven decisions
commonGaps: No model risk framework for AI/ML applications
Models deployed without validation
Bias not assessed for client-facing AI
No explainability for AI-driven trading or compliance decisions
evidenceItems.format: Model documentation / Validation reports / Bias assessments

evidenceItems.name: DLT and Digital Asset Control Records
evidenceItems.description: Smart contract security assessments, key management and wallet control procedures, regulatory compliance evidence for digital asset activities, and DLT infrastructure monitoring records.
whatGoodLooksLike: Smart contract security assessments before deployment
Secure key management with HSM and multi-signature controls
SC digital asset guidelines compliance documented
DLT infrastructure monitored for security and performance
commonGaps: No smart contract security assessment process
Key management relies on software wallets without HSM
Regulatory compliance for digital assets not assessed
No DLT-specific monitoring
evidenceItems.format: Security assessment reports / Key management procedures / Compliance records

evidenceItems.name: SC Advisory Compliance Tracker
evidenceItems.description: Tracker showing compliance with SC advisories on algorithmic trading, AI governance, and emerging technology, including gap analysis and remediation actions.
whatGoodLooksLike: All relevant SC advisories identified and tracked
Gap analysis conducted for each advisory
Remediation actions assigned and tracked to closure
Board or committee informed of advisory compliance status
commonGaps: SC advisories not monitored systematically
No gap analysis against advisories
Remediation actions not defined
Board not informed of advisory compliance
